description: Resident FPGA↔IDE I/O channel. Self-identifying media channel: every packet carries a 4-byte ASCII FourCC type tag (open registry—add tags without changing dispatch code). Register replaces the hardwired B:02½ call-home boot step. Call(GT) forwards through the tunnel to a remote capability (the ‘Hello Mum’ primitive).

methods.name: Register
methods.description: Send the 23-byte call-home identification packet [0xCE11 · board_type · fw_version · HMAC-SHA256(4B) · UID(8B) · boot_reason · last_fault · fault_NIA(4B)] and await ACK from the IDE bridge. Replaces the hardwired B:02½ boot step. Offline-safe: if no ACK within ≈500 ms, DR0 ← 0 and boot continues.
outputs: DR0: 1 = IDE connected and ACK received | 0 = offline

methods.name: Send
methods.description: Transmit a self-identifying media packet to the IDE host. DR1 is a 4-byte ASCII FourCC type tag (open registry): TEXT=0x54455854 · VOIC=0x564F4943 · LUMP=0x4C554D50 · GTKN=0x47544B4E · JPEG=0x4A504547 · PDF_=0x50444620 · MP3_=0x4D503320 · … New tags can be allocated without touching existing dispatch code. Fire-and-forget; no ACK.
inputs: DR1: FourCC type tag (4-byte ASCII, open registry)

methods.name: Receive
methods.description: Block until the IDE host sends a self-identifying media packet or the timeout expires. The caller dispatches on the returned FourCC type tag (DR1). New media types are handled by extending the caller’s dispatch table—no change to Receive itself.
inputs: DR1: timeout in CPU steps (0 = wait forever)
outputs: DR1: FourCC type tag (TEXT · VOIC · LUMP · GTKN · …)

methods.name: Fault
methods.description: Report a hardware fault with full semantic location to the IDE. High-priority: bypasses the normal send queue. Packet format: [0xFA17 · fault_code · ns_idx · thread_gt · abstr_idx · method_idx · instr_offset]. IDE logs the full location in the Devices view. Fire-and-forget.
inputs: DR3: thread_gt — NS index of the faulting thread
DR4: abstr_idx — NS slot of the executing abstraction
DR5: method_idx — method index within the abstraction
DR6: instr_offset — instruction offset within the method

methods.name: Fetch
methods.description: Request and receive a lump binary from the IDE by NS slot token. Validates the incoming lump (magic=0x1F, size, CRC-16) before writing to memory and updating the NS entry. Called by Loader when a NULL_CAP fault hits a cold slot. Packet: [0xFE7C · token · expected_words].
inputs: DR1: NS slot token (index of requested abstraction)
DR2: expected lump size in words

methods.name: Call
methods.description: Forward a CALL through the tunnel to a remote capability. Encodes the GT in CR2 as a GTKN packet (tag=0x47544B4E), transmits it, and blocks awaiting the far-end RETURN. This is the ‘Hello Mum’ primitive: CALL(CONNECT(me, mymother)). From the caller’s view this is one Church instruction applied across two machines.
inputs: CR2: remote GT (Outform type, F-bit set — far-end abstraction)
outputs: DR0: far-end return value | non-zero = error / timeout
